headlines.title: Trump Wraps China Summit With Xi Jinping: What Are the Results?
headlines.description: President Donald Trump is concluding his high-stakes visit to China and is already declaring it a success. The summit’s results so far are still unclear with no announcements of any new trade deals or purchases of American products. Trump suggested that airplane maker Boeing struck a deal to sell more aircrafts to Chinese companies, but offered few details. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports and moderator of Meet the Press Kristin Welker joins TODAY with analysis.

headlines.title: Inside China’s race to dominate humanoid robotics
headlines.description: Tom Llamas goes inside a Beijing robot plant as China’s race to build autonomous humanoids accelerates, raising new questions about whether the U.S. can keep pace in the next generation of robotics.

headlines.title: Pandas set for 8,000-mile blind date in Atlanta
headlines.description: They’ve never met, but Ping Ping and Fu Shuang are getting ready for the blind date of a lifetime.

headlines.title: Families of Americans imprisoned in China appeal to Trump for help
headlines.description: After living without their loved ones for more than a decade, two families of Americans detained in China are appealing to President Donald Trump to bring them home as he meets with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing.

headlines.title: Few deals from Trump’s trip and no progress on Iran send global markets into a sell-off
headlines.description: Government bonds around the world sold off sharply Friday as the price of oil jumped, both driven by developments in the Iran war, politics and trade.

headlines.title: DOJ pushing to indict Raúl Castro over 1996 downing of civilian planes, officials say
headlines.description: The Justice Department is pushing to indict 94-year-old Raúl Castro, the former president of Cuba, according to two U.S. officials familiar with the matter.

headlines.title: Judge orders Harvey Weinstein jury to continue deliberations after being deadlocked
headlines.description: Jurors in the third sex crimes trial against Harvey Weinstein were ordered continue deliberating after telling the judge they were deadlocked and could not reach a unanimous decision. NBC News' Chloe Melas explains that if a verdict cannot be reached, the court will need to schedule a new trial in front of a different jury.

headlines.title: Families caring for disabled relatives face unthinkable choices as Medicaid cuts loom
headlines.description: President Donald Trump’s Medicaid cuts and emerging attacks on paid family caregiving have people fearing financial ruin as they fight to keep loved ones out of institutions.

headlines.title: AI-generated video supporting Spencer Pratt for mayor of L.A. goes viral adding to concern over fake campaign ads
headlines.description: Republican mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt reposted an AI-generated video depicting him as a Batman-inspired character saving a burning Los Angeles from his opponents, raising questions about how AI is reshaping elections. NBC News’ Steve Patterson reports.

headlines.title: YouTube taught a Japanese teen how to kick field goals. Now he’s in the NFL.
headlines.description: Kansei Matsuzawa, 27, became the first Japanese-born player ever signed by an NFL team when he agreed to a deal as an undrafted free agent with the Raiders.

headlines.title: The evolution of album rollouts
headlines.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

headlines.title: Georgia Senate race tests Gov. Brian Kemp’s sway in the Republican Party
headlines.description: With his time in the governor’s office coming to an end, Brian Kemp has asked Georgia voters for “one last thing.”“Send Derek Dooley to the U.S.
